message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): resolve_tls_versions positive-path contracts (tick 339)

Added three tests pinning that `build_tls_bundle` accepts each
supported `min_tls_version` config string:

- `build_tls_bundle_accepts_min_tls_version_1_2` — "1.2" builds cleanly
- `build_tls_bundle_accepts_min_tls_version_1_3` — "1.3" builds cleanly
- `build_tls_bundle_accepts_min_tls_version_1_2_plus_suffix` — "1.2+"
  suffix form (explicitly permitted per distributed.rs L122-L123)
  round-trips identically to the bare "1.2" form

Complements tick 335's negative test (`resolve_tls_versions_rejects_
unsupported_version_string`) which pins that `"2.0"` → `InvalidMinTlsVersion`.

Why this matters: min-TLS-version is a security-relevant knob.
Setting it to "1.3" must mean the server refuses TLS 1.2 clients; "1.2"
accepts both. A regression that silently collapsed both into a single
version list would either:
- weaken security (if "1.3" downgraded to include 1.2), or
- break compatibility (if "1.2" upgraded to exclude 1.2).

The positive tests pin that the explicit accepted-version strings
build a valid TLS bundle, so any future refactor that changes the
parser must either preserve these inputs or explicitly break them.

Scope note: these tests pin that the *config surface* accepts the
valid inputs. Actually verifying that "1.3" refuses a TLS 1.2
handshake would require wire-level testing against a fixed client —
out of scope for unit tests. The negative test (version parser
rejects unknown inputs) + positive tests (version parser accepts
known inputs) together bound the behavior.

Test result: 3 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 18s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
